conflicts.dxmSsri: ⛔ DXM CANNOT be taken with SSRI/SNRI — serotonin syndrome!

conflicts.halfDoseWarning: With bupropion: HALF dose of aripiprazole/brexpiprazole
conflicts.fluoxetineWarning: Fluoxetine: very long T½, potent CYP2D6 inhibitor

profilePage.geneticsDesc: CYP enzymes, MTHFR, COMT, BDNF, 5-HTTLPR, HLA polymorphisms that affect drug metabolism and response.
profilePage.symptomsDesc: PHQ-9, GAD-7, PCL-5, ASRS standardized symptom assessments with score tracking over time.
profilePage.labsDesc: Blood panels, hormones, inflammatory markers, and other lab results with reference ranges.
profilePage.treatmentDesc: Past and current medications, doses, durations, and response outcomes.
profilePage.uploadDesc: Upload Apple Health XML, PDF lab reports, or other clinical documents.

geneticForm.title: Genetic Profile
geneticForm.description: CYP enzymes, MTHFR, COMT, BDNF, 5-HTTLPR, HLA polymorphisms that affect drug metabolism and response.
geneticForm.cyp2d6Hint: Metabolizer status: PM (poor), IM (intermediate), EM (extensive/normal), UM (ultra-rapid)
geneticForm.cyp2c19Hint: Affects citalopram, escitalopram, sertraline, clopidogrel metabolism
geneticForm.mthfrHint: TT homozygote — reduced folate metabolism, may need L-methylfolate
geneticForm.comtHint: Met/Met — slow COMT, higher DA/NE levels. Val/Val — fast COMT
geneticForm.bdnfHint: Met carriers — reduced activity-dependent BDNF secretion
geneticForm.httlprHint: S/S — reduced SERT expression, slower SSRI response, higher anxiety risk
geneticForm.hlaB1502Hint: Risk for carbamazepine-induced SJS/TEN (screen in Asian populations)

symptomForm.title: Symptom Scales
symptomForm.description: Standardized symptom assessments. Select a scale to begin the questionnaire.
symptomForm.question: Question
symptomForm.phq9_q1: Little interest or pleasure in doing things
symptomForm.phq9_q3: Trouble falling or staying asleep, or sleeping too much
symptomForm.phq9_q4: Feeling tired or having little energy
symptomForm.phq9_q6: Feeling bad about yourself — or that you are a failure or have let yourself or your family down
symptomForm.phq9_q7: Trouble concentrating on things, such as reading the newspaper or watching television
symptomForm.phq9_q8: Moving or speaking so slowly that other people could have noticed? Or the opposite — being so fidgety or restless
symptomForm.phq9_q9: Thoughts that you would be better off dead, or of hurting yourself in some way
symptomForm.gad7_q1: Feeling nervous, anxious, or on edge
symptomForm.gad7_q2: Not being able to stop or control worrying
symptomForm.gad7_q3: Worrying too much about different things
symptomForm.gad7_q5: Being so restless that it's hard to sit still
symptomForm.gad7_q7: Feeling afraid, as if something awful might happen
symptomForm.pcl5_q1: Repeated, disturbing, and unwanted memories of the stressful experience
symptomForm.pcl5_q2: Repeated, disturbing dreams of the stressful experience
symptomForm.pcl5_q3: Suddenly feeling or acting as if the stressful experience were actually happening again
symptomForm.pcl5_q4: Feeling very upset when something reminded you of the stressful experience
symptomForm.pcl5_q5: Having strong physical reactions when something reminded you (e.g., heart pounding, trouble breathing)
symptomForm.pcl5_q6: Avoiding memories, thoughts, or feelings related to the stressful experience
symptomForm.pcl5_q7: Avoiding external reminders (e.g., people, places, conversations, activities, objects, situations)
symptomForm.pcl5_q8: Trouble remembering important parts of the stressful experience
symptomForm.pcl5_q9: Having strong negative beliefs about yourself, other people, or the world
symptomForm.pcl5_q10: Blaming yourself or someone else for the stressful experience or what happened after it
symptomForm.pcl5_q11: Having strong negative feelings such as fear, horror, anger, guilt, or shame
symptomForm.pcl5_q12: Loss of interest in activities that you used to enjoy
symptomForm.pcl5_q13: Feeling distant or cut off from other people
symptomForm.pcl5_q14: Trouble experiencing positive feelings (e.g., being unable to feel happiness or love)
symptomForm.pcl5_q15: Irritable behavior, angry outbursts, or acting aggressively
symptomForm.pcl5_q16: Taking too many risks or doing things that could cause you harm
symptomForm.pcl5_q17: Being 'superalert' or watchful or on guard
symptomForm.asrs_q1: How often do you have trouble wrapping up the final details of a project, once the challenging parts have been done?
symptomForm.asrs_q2: How often do you have difficulty getting things in order when you have to do a task that requires organization?
symptomForm.asrs_q3: How often do you have problems remembering appointments or obligations?
symptomForm.asrs_q4: When you have a task that requires a lot of thought, how often do you avoid or delay getting started?
symptomForm.asrs_q5: How often do you fidget or squirm with your hands or feet when you have to sit down for a long time?
symptomForm.asrs_q6: How often do you feel overly active and compelled to do things, like you were driven by a motor?
symptomForm.asrs_q7: How often do you make careless mistakes when you have to work on a boring or difficult project?
symptomForm.asrs_q8: How often do you have difficulty keeping your attention when you are doing boring or repetitive work?
symptomForm.asrs_q9: How often do you have difficulty concentrating on what people say to you, even when they are speaking to you directly?
symptomForm.asrs_q10: How often do you misplace or have difficulty finding things at home or at work?
symptomForm.asrs_q11: How often are you distracted by activity or noise around you?
symptomForm.asrs_q12: How often do you leave your seat in meetings or other situations in which you are expected to remain seated?
symptomForm.asrs_q13: How often do you feel restless or fidgety?
symptomForm.asrs_q14: How often do you have difficulty unwinding and relaxing when you have time to yourself?
symptomForm.asrs_q15: How often do you find yourself talking too much when you are in social situations?
symptomForm.asrs_q16: When you're in a conversation, how often do you find yourself finishing the sentences of the people you are talking to?
symptomForm.asrs_q17: How often do you have difficulty waiting your turn in situations when turn taking is required?
symptomForm.asrs_q18: How often do you interrupt others when they are busy?
